speaker: MACBETH
text: Why should I play the Roman fool and die

speaker: MACBETH
text: On mine own sword? Whiles I see lives, the gashes

speaker: MACBETH
text: Do better upon them.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Turn, hellhound, turn!

speaker: MACBETH
text: Of all men else I have avoided thee.

speaker: MACBETH
text: But get thee back. My soul is too much charged

speaker: MACBETH
text: With blood of thine already.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: I have no words;

speaker: MACDUFF
text: My voice is in my sword, thou bloodier villain

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Than terms can give thee out.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Thou losest labor.

speaker: MACBETH
text: As easy mayst thou the intrenchant air

speaker: MACBETH
text: With thy keen sword impress as make me bleed.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Let fall thy blade on vulnerable crests;

speaker: MACBETH
text: I bear a charmèd life, which must not yield

speaker: MACBETH
text: To one of woman born.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Despair thy charm,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: And let the angel whom thou still hast served

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Tell thee Macduff was from his mother’s womb

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Untimely ripped.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Accursèd be that tongue that tells me so,

speaker: MACBETH
text: For it hath cowed my better part of man!

speaker: MACBETH
text: And be these juggling fiends no more believed

speaker: MACBETH
text: That palter with us in a double sense,

speaker: MACBETH
text: That keep the word of promise to our ear

speaker: MACBETH
text: And break it to our hope. I’ll not fight with thee.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Then yield thee, coward,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: And live to be the show and gaze o’ th’ time.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: We’ll have thee, as our rarer monsters are,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Painted upon a pole, and underwrit

speaker: MACDUFF
text: “Here may you see the tyrant.”

speaker: MACBETH
text: I will not yield

speaker: MACBETH
text: To kiss the ground before young Malcolm’s feet

speaker: MACBETH
text: And to be baited with the rabble’s curse.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Though Birnam Wood be come to Dunsinane

speaker: MACBETH
text: And thou opposed, being of no woman born,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Yet I will try the last. Before my body

speaker: MACBETH
text: I throw my warlike shield. Lay on, Macduff,

speaker: MACBETH
text: And damned be him that first cries “Hold! Enough!”

speaker: MALCOLM
text: I would the friends we miss were safe arrived.

speaker: SIWARD
text: Some must go off; and yet by these I see

speaker: SIWARD
text: So great a day as this is cheaply bought.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Macduff is missing, and your noble son.

speaker: ROSS
text: Your son, my lord, has paid a soldier’s debt.

speaker: ROSS
text: He only lived but till he was a man,

speaker: ROSS
text: The which no sooner had his prowess confirmed

speaker: ROSS
text: In the unshrinking station where he fought,

speaker: ROSS
text: But like a man he died.

speaker: SIWARD
text: Then he is dead?

speaker: ROSS
text: Ay, and brought off the field. Your cause of sorrow

speaker: ROSS
text: Must not be measured by his worth, for then

speaker: ROSS
text: It hath no end.

speaker: SIWARD
text: Had he his hurts before?

speaker: ROSS
text: Ay, on the front.

speaker: SIWARD
text: Why then, God’s soldier be he!

speaker: SIWARD
text: Had I as many sons as I have hairs,

speaker: SIWARD
text: I would not wish them to a fairer death;

speaker: SIWARD
text: And so his knell is knolled.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: He’s worth more sorrow, and that I’ll spend for him.

speaker: SIWARD
text: He’s worth no more.

speaker: SIWARD
text: They say he parted well and paid his score,

speaker: SIWARD
text: And so, God be with him. Here comes newer comfort.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Hail, King! for so thou art. Behold where stands

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Th’ usurper’s cursèd head. The time is free.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: I see thee compassed with thy kingdom’s pearl,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: That speak my salutation in their minds,

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Whose voices I desire aloud with mine.

speaker: MACDUFF
text: Hail, King of Scotland!

speaker: ALL
text: Hail, King of Scotland!

speaker: MALCOLM
text: We shall not spend a large expense of time

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Before we reckon with your several loves

speaker: MALCOLM
text: And make us even with you. My thanes and kinsmen,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Henceforth be earls, the first that ever Scotland

speaker: MALCOLM
text: In such an honor named. What’s more to do,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Which would be planted newly with the time,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: As calling home our exiled friends abroad

speaker: MALCOLM
text: That fled the snares of watchful tyranny,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Producing forth the cruel ministers

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Of this dead butcher and his fiend-like queen

speaker: MALCOLM
text: (Who, as ’tis thought, by self and violent hands,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Took off her life)—this, and what needful else

speaker: MALCOLM
text: That calls upon us, by the grace of grace,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: We will perform in measure, time, and place.

speaker: MALCOLM
text: So thanks to all at once and to each one,

speaker: MALCOLM
text: Whom we invite to see us crowned at Scone.
